sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 65

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 65:1
items.html: At the time of the Nukva's Katnut, ZA [Zeir Anpin] and the Nukva are found cleaving in one wall. The meaning is: the Nukva-in-the-body of ZA, which serves ZA himself and is the discernment of his wall — namely, the discernment of the Masach [screen] and Aviut [coarseness] that raises Returning Light, as is known — with it the separated Nukva also serves now, receiving the discernments of the Masachim in the Nukva-in-the-body of ZA. For at the time of her Ibur [conception], the Nukva-in-the-body of ZA awakens and renews the Masach of Ibur that was in her at the time of the Ibur; and likewise in Yenika [suckling] she renews her own Masach from the time of Yenika, and imparts the Lights to the separated Nukva, as is known. Thus the separated Nukva serves and receives her abundance by means of the Nukva-in-the-body of ZA, which is the discernment of the wall of ZA himself. For as long as the Nukva has no Peh of Rosh — which is the place of Zivug [coupling] of every Partzuf — she is obliged to be included in the Zivug of the great ZON, that is, in the Nukva-in-his-body, which measures out to her, by the power of her Zivug with ZA, all the corresponding discernments in which the separated Nukva stands. And this resembles the embryo and the suckling infant, which eats what its mother eats — meaning, through inclusion in the Zivug in its mother, in her Peh of Rosh, for only the Peh is the root place of Zivug. Therefore even the Gevurot that the Nukva receives in her days of Ibur and Yenika are only illuminations of the Gevurot and not the essence of the Gevurot, as is known — because she lacks the GAR of the Gevurot, which are called Binot.

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 65:2
items.html: (to the question) But at the time of the Nesira [sawing apart], when the Nukva received the discernments of the Masach of Gadlut of the Nukva-in-the-body of ZA, and the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey] descended from her Eynaim [eyes], and the fallen AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh] returned to her — so that she has a complete Partzuf of Rosh, Toch, Sof, exactly like the Nukva-in-the-body of ZA, for her level is now equal with ZA up to his Keter — it is discerned that the Nukva now comprises all the discernments, from Rosh to Sof, that were in the Nukva-in-the-body. Therefore, from the Zivug she has now acquired at the place of the Daat, which is the discernment of the Peh of Rosh, she now has the discernments of GAR of the Rosh belonging to the Nukva, called actual Binot, and also the discernments of the Gevurot of the Guf, which are now the essence of the Gevurot and not their illumination as at first — such that there is now no difference whatsoever between the separated Nukva and the Nukva-in-the-body of ZA. And this is what the Rav says: at the time of the Nesira, the separated Nukva takes the whole BON side of ZA — that is, his whole Partzuf of Binot and Gevurot, called the Nukva-in-his-body, or the great Nukva. Accordingly, the discernments of the NHY of Ima that were clothed in the great Nukva of ZA are now clothed in the separated Nukva, for she joins with her into one Partzuf. (Item 109)
